portfolio_rationale: GOOGL and AAPL lead with the strongest combination of fundamentals and technicals, where the bear cases were weakest and bull cases most compelling. AMZN earns a meaningful allocation on superior revenue growth and clean technicals, while BRK.B and XOM receive smaller positions given mixed technical signals and weaker fundamental stories. LLY is excluded entirely due to a bearish judge verdict with deteriorating technicals.

GOOGL.reason: Strongest bull case with clean technicals (RSI 68, 10% 20-day return), very low leverage, high ROE, and a bear case that was factually weak on the debt argument.

AAPL.reason: Excellent technical momentum (RSI 68, strong MACD, 9.8% 20-day return), robust margins and FCF, with liquidity concerns immaterial given Apple's cash generation.

AMZN.reason: Best revenue growth in the universe (13.3%), strong margins, and clean technicals with RSI at neutral 48 suggesting more upside room than AAPL/GOOGL.

BRK.B.reason: Solid profitability and positive news flow support a small position, but negative revenue growth and mixed EMA signals limit conviction.

XOM.reason: Positive news sentiment and solid FCF warrant a small allocation, but bearish technical analyst signal with price below 20-day MA and negative 20-day momentum cap the position.

LLY.reason: Judge signal is bearish with deteriorating technicals (RSI 33, price below 20-day MA, negative 20-day momentum) and elevated leverage risk.
